results.possibleIdentity: Allyn S. Rashkin (1939–1977) was an engineer and technical professional active during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the GAF Corporation (General Aniline & Film), specifically associated with their facility in Vails Gate, New York, which was part of the company's industrial and technical operations (including the former Ruberoid plant). Rashkin's expertise was broad, covering mathematics, physics, optics, spectroscopy, and fluorescence, along with photography and electronics. He was a registered engineer with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded to facilitate collaborations between artists and the technical community. His technical background in color instrumentation, ultraviolet light, and fiber optics aligned with GAF's business in chemicals, film, and imaging technologies. Records indicate he was born in 1939 and died in June 1977 in Newark, New Jersey.
